headlines.title: Fort Worth Chamber of Commerce to Discuss Magnolia Franchise Today
headlines.fullText: A meeting of the Chamber of Commerce will be held at 11:30 o'clock this morning to discuss the proposed granting of a franchise for a pipe line through the streets of Fort Worth, The Magnolia Petroleum Company is the applicant. Mr. Paul Waples will address the meeting regarding the franchise. The directors of the Chamber of Commerce are asked to be present.

headlines.title: Y.M.B.L. Probes Magnolia Franchise; Independent Company Asks Rights of Competition in City
headlines.subtitle: Ready to Boost Fort Worth Fight Opens to Block Exclusive Grant; New Concern Proposes $3,000,000 Refinery for City
headlines.fullText:
BY FRANK S. TILLMAN.
Affairs in the proposed granting of a twenty-five year pipe line franchise in Fort Worth to the Magnolia Petroleum company came to a crisis yesterday. Two open charges of a "nigger in the wood pile" are made.
The Young Men's Business league, at a meeting last night, asked for a report on the details of the application. The point raised by the organization is that the proposed franchise would, in all probability, grant an exclusive right to the Magnolia Petroleum company to use the streets of Fort Worth for twenty-five years; would allow the company to start operations at any time, or delay them as long as they wished, thus denying the right of any other company that so desired to pipe oil through the city streets on a competative basis.
The Chamber of Commerce directors are scheduled to meet this morning to listen to an address by Paul Waples on the franchise matter, according to a call sent out yesterday.
Independent oil interests of Buffalo and Pittsburg, backed by men of undoubted financial ability, are anxious to develope great fields near Fort Worth. They wish the right to pipe line into and through the city, build a refinery, but do not dare go ahead at present in the face of the proposed exclusive granting of the city streets to the Magnolia interests. . They have been working quietly in Fort Worth and vicinity for weeks,/and within the past three days have laid their case before the Fort Worth Record.

headlines.title: JOLLY AD MEN ON THEIR JOURNEY TO TORONTO MEETING
headlines.subtitle: 60 In Party As Special Leaves City
headlines.fullText:
Sacred Heifers, Emblematic of Fort Worth's Leading Industry, Will Add to City's Laurels at Convention.
With buoyant expectancy written on their faces, members of the Fort Worth Ad club, some accompanied by their wives and children, departed Wednesday night in "The Texas Publicity Special" for Toronto, to attend the annual convention of the National Association of Advertising Clubs of America, which opens in that city Sunday, June 21. The club members were distinguished by Kelly green hat bands and neckties, the official color for the trip. There were nearly sixty in the party as the train pulled out of the station. .. headlines.title: Publicity Of Franchise Is Demanded
headlines.fullText:
A committee of five members of the Young Men's Business league was authorized at the regular meeting of the league Wednesday night to look into the application and the proposed ordinance filed with the application by the Magnolia Petroleum company asking a twenty-five year pipe line franchise from the city of Fort Worth.

headlines.title: HUERTA DELEGATES GIVE OUT STATEMENT EXPLAINING WHY DEADLOCK ON TERMS OF SETTLEMENT CAME ABOUT.
headlines.subtitle: If Constitutionalist Is Named Fraud and Violence Will Follow
headlines.fullText:
NIAGARA FALLS, Ont., June 17-The Huerta delegation the mediation conference issued a statement tonight charging that the insistence by the United States on a constitutionalist for the provisional presidency as opposed to a "neutral" was "tantamount to abetting and even exacting fraud and violence at the elections.
